_risk_level_doc: high: provenance gate is a QA correctness gate for the entire harness; gaps allow under-reporting and missing provenance fields to go undetected across all dev cycles

requirement.original: Three dev provenance completeness gaps found by Codex in cycle 20260522-080646: (D1) QA only checks over-reporting, not under-reporting; (D2) parallel aggregate dev-report missing baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, observed_preexisting; (D3) files_created derivation misses staged new files
requirement.clarified: Fix three gaps in agents/qa.md, commands/dev.md, and agents/dev.md that together allowed a dev-report claiming 2 files_modified when the git diff showed 4 to pass QA undetected
requirement.what: Procedural text additions/changes to three markdown files: (1) add QA reverse-direction under-report check, (2) add three provenance fields to the parallel-dev aggregate spec, (3) extend files_created derivation to include staged new files
requirement.why: Cycle 20260522-080646 item D demonstrated the gaps: QA passed despite dev under-reporting modified files; provenance fields dropped from aggregate would cause provenance checks to skip for any parallel-dev cycle; staged new files are silently omitted from files_created

included: agents/qa.md: Step 5 provenance check — add reverse (under-reporting) check after current step 5
agents/qa.md: Step 5 files_created check (step 6-9) — extend git command to include staged-added files
commands/dev.md:699 — summary union rule bullet for provenance fields
commands/dev.md:720-734 — detailed aggregate construction rule for three provenance fields
commands/dev.md:739-756 — example aggregate JSON to include baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, dev.observed_preexisting
agents/dev.md:495 — extend files_created derivation to union untracked + staged-added files
excluded: agents/changelog-analyst.md: mirrors same D3 gap but is path-external to this fix scope
Schema enforcement tooling or validation scripts
Per-worker dev-report schema (fields already exist; no per-worker changes needed)
Any changes to commit.sh or close.md

success_criteria: QA raises files_modified_underreport_violation for files in git diff absent from dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created and absent from baseline_dirty_paths
Aggregate dev-report includes baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, dev.observed_preexisting; aggregate blocked if workers disagree on baseline values
files_created derivation unions git ls-files --others --exclude-standard with git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A; QA check mirrors the same combined set
constraints: All changes are procedural text additions to existing markdown files — no new scripts
observed_preexisting must NOT be used as an exclusion in the D1 reverse check (it is informational and cannot logically contain diff-present files per agents/dev.md:499)
baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot in aggregate must come from orchestrator dispatch value, not worker union
dev.observed_preexisting in aggregate is UNION of per-worker values (it is a list, not a scalar)
D1 reverse check must compare diff_files against files_modified UNION files_created (not files_modified alone) to avoid false positives on staged new files that appear in both lists

out_of_scope_observations.observation: changelog-analyst.md:127 mirrors the incomplete git ls-files --others check for files_created provenance anomaly detection — the same gap as D3 but in a different agent path. This is outside the user-stated fix scope (user named agents/qa.md, commands/dev.md, agents/dev.md). Logging for cross-cycle visibility.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Cycle 20260522-080646 item D dev-report claimed files_modified=[2 files]; git diff showed 4 files changed; QA issued pass verdict
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Three independent gaps: (1) QA provenance check only iterates dev.files_modified against diff (over-report direction); no iteration of diff against dev.files_modified (under-report direction); (2) parallel-dev aggregate construction rule omits three provenance fields QA depends on; (3) files_created derivation command misses staged-but-not-committed new files
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — gaps in procedural spec text added during cycle 20260522-080646-D, not a regression from a prior working state
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: The original provenance check (cycle 20260522-080646-D) addressed fabrication (claiming to modify files you did not touch) but did not address omission (failing to list files you did touch). Aggregate spec was not updated in parallel. files_created used a standard untracked-files command without considering the staged state.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Under-reporting allows dev to silently scope-restrict its declared footprint. Missing aggregate fields cause the provenance check to skip or produce false exclusions for all parallel-dev cycles. Missed staged files cause both dev and QA to be blind to a class of new files.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Gap introduced with cycle 20260522-080646-D; first demonstrated by the dev-report for that same cycle (2 vs 4 files_modified); confirmed by Codex post-cycle review.
observations: agents/qa.md lines 411-422: iterates dev.files_modified against diff — no reverse iteration (inferred from file read)
agents/qa.md lines 427-435: uses git ls-files --others --exclude-standard only — no git diff --cached --diff-filter=A (inferred from file read)
commands/dev.md lines 720-734: construction rule lists fields to union; baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, dev.observed_preexisting absent (inferred from file read)
commands/dev.md lines 739-756: example JSON body has no baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, or observed_preexisting fields (inferred from file read)
commands/dev.md line 699: summary union rule bullet omits provenance fields (inferred from file read)
agents/dev.md line 495: files_created derivation uses git ls-files --others --exclude-standard exclusively (inferred from file read)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — runtime QA pass on cycle 20260522-080646-D is cited as observed evidence from cycle history but not re-observed in this session)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: grep and Read on three files confirmed exact line ranges for all gaps

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: not a regression — gaps exist in spec text introduced in cycle 20260522-080646-D; no prior working state to bisect to

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: QA only checks over-reporting ... does NOT check the reverse: files in the git diff that are NOT in files_modified
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: QA raises files_modified_underreport_violation (severity: critical) for any file in git diff --name-only <baseline> that is absent from dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created and absent from baseline_dirty_paths

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: parallel aggregate missing provenance fields: baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, observed_preexisting
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Aggregate dev-report JSON contains top-level baseline_head_sha, top-level baseline_dirty_snapshot (both equality-verified from orchestrator dispatch value; aggregate blocked if any worker differs), and nested dev.observed_preexisting (UNION of per-worker lists); both the construction rule text and the example JSON are updated

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: files_created misses staged new files: git ls-files --others ... staged (git add) are not 'others'
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: files_created derivation is UNION of (git ls-files --others --exclude-standard) and (git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A), minus baseline_dirty_paths; QA's check mirrors the same combined set

component_chain.role: defines mandatory derivation rules for files_modified, files_created, baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, observed_preexisting in per-worker dev-report
component_chain.evidence: Read agents/dev.md lines 490-503 confirmed all five field definitions

component_chain.role: defines how orchestrator aggregates N per-worker dev-reports into canonical aggregate; currently missing provenance fields
component_chain.evidence: Read commands/dev.md lines 720-756 confirmed absent fields; grep confirmed lines 699 summary also omits them

component_chain.role: QA Step 5 provenance check reads aggregate (or single-dev) dev-report and verifies provenance in both directions (currently only one direction); also checks files_created using incomplete command
component_chain.evidence: Read agents/qa.md lines 405-438 confirmed: (1) no reverse check exists, (2) files_created check uses ls-files --others only

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: If `baseline_head_sha` is absent or empty, skip this check and log: `WARNING: baseline_head_sha absent — provenance check skipped`
pre_existing_guards.purpose: guards against false failures in unborn-repo or pre-first-commit cycles where no baseline SHA exists

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Paths that appear in `baseline_dirty_snapshot` are excluded from the FAIL set
pre_existing_guards.purpose: guards against false positives for files that were already dirty at baseline time (pre-existing modifications dev did not introduce)

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Paths that appear in `baseline_dirty_snapshot` are excluded from the FAIL set even if absent from the untracked list
pre_existing_guards.purpose: same baseline-dirty exclusion for the files_created direction

setup.reason: non-UI -- agent-prompt edit; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

evidence.observed: Cycle 20260522-080646 item D dev-report claimed 2 files in files_modified; git diff against baseline showed 4 files changed; QA still passed

measured.value: agents/qa.md lines 411-422 iterate dev.files_modified against diff_files; no code iterating diff_files against dev.files_modified exists. commands/dev.md lines 720-756 construction rule lists 9 union fields, all omitting baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, dev.observed_preexisting. agents/dev.md:495 specifies git ls-files --others --exclude-standard with no mention of git diff --cached.

expected.value: Reverse check present in QA; three provenance fields in aggregate spec and example JSON; files_created derivation unions untracked and staged-added files
expected.source: User requirement document + Codex review findings

evidence.gap: Three independent omissions in procedural spec text: (1) no reverse under-report check, (2) aggregate spec missing 3 fields, (3) files_created derivation missing one git command

reference_source.description: File reads of agents/qa.md, commands/dev.md, agents/dev.md in this session; git man page semantics for diff --name-only, ls-files --others, diff --cached --diff-filter=A verified against known git behavior

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempt exists for this specific set of gaps

context.codebase_state: master branch at f79fba1c1684d246928a3cfb39bf1ed7a9a5c8a5 (baseline); large working tree dirty with prior-cycle artifacts
context.recent_commits: aea75d9 chore(docs): ship cycle 20260522-000000 close-report and QA verification; 5771880 feat(spec): commit spec-20260520-221059 and split views to git (arch-2); af96c61 fix(infra): M1-M4 gitignore exceptions, install mirror, uncapped_delta

development_approach.strategy: Three targeted text additions to existing markdown procedural sections. No new files. No new scripts. Additive-only changes.
development_approach.validation_approach: QA verifies: (1) agents/qa.md contains new reverse-check prose with files_modified_underreport_violation label; (2) commands/dev.md aggregate section contains baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot at top level and dev.observed_preexisting in construction rule and example JSON; (3) agents/dev.md:495 mentions git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A in the files_created derivation

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Iter-1 (7 findings): observed_preexisting misuse as QA exclusion; staged-addition disjoint clarification; observed_preexisting nesting under dev; baseline mismatch should block not warn; baseline_dirty_snapshot is a scalar not unioned; both commands/dev.md sections need patching; porcelain parsing must be explicit. Iter-2 (4 findings with PROPOSED_FIX + 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY): (1) AC5 AND clause still uses bare 'absent from files_modified' — needs union form; (2) Evidence Expected text and context success_criteria[0] also use bare form; (3) acceptance-criteria JSON needs standard top-level envelope; (4) files_to_create in context must include the new AC JSON artifact; (5) OBSERVATION_ONLY: AC6 as proposed precisely tests I1 union-exclusion boundary.

findings.issue: D1 draft allowed observed_preexisting as a QA exclusion in the reverse check — but agents/dev.md:499 defines it as informational for paths NOT in git diff, so it cannot logically contain diff-present files; using it as an exclusion creates an escape hatch
findings.proposed_fix: Reverse check excludes only baseline_dirty_paths (parsed from baseline_dirty_snapshot porcelain). May emit a non-blocking warning if observed_preexisting intersects diff_files - baseline_dirty_paths.
findings.rationale: The escape-hatch concern is valid and precise. AC5 now tests that observed_preexisting is NOT an exclusion in the reverse check.

findings.issue: D1 and D3 need explicit statement that staged added paths may appear in both files_modified and files_created (non-disjoint lists); D1 reverse check should compare against files_modified ∪ files_created
findings.proposed_fix: Add wording that the two lists are not required to be disjoint; D1 reverse check uses diff_files vs (files_modified ∪ files_created) to avoid false positives on staged new files
findings.rationale: git diff --name-only with default diff-filter includes A (added) files, so a staged new file appears in diff and in files_created. Without this, AC1's reverse check would incorrectly raise violations for staged new files not in files_modified. S1 and AC1 wording updated.

findings.issue: D2 draft said observed_preexisting is a top-level field in the aggregate; actual schema has it nested under dev (agents/dev.md:521: dev.observed_preexisting)
findings.proposed_fix: Aggregate dev.observed_preexisting under the dev object, matching per-worker schema
findings.rationale: The schema position matters for QA and downstream readers. spec and context JSON updated to say dev.observed_preexisting (nested).

findings.issue: D2 draft suggested log-warning on baseline_head_sha mismatch across workers; Codex says this is provenance corruption, not a warning
findings.proposed_fix: Mark aggregate blocked if any worker's baseline_head_sha differs from the orchestrator dispatch value
findings.rationale: A baseline mismatch means at least one worker ran against a different commit than intended; the aggregate cannot be trusted for provenance checks. Blocking is the correct severity.

findings.issue: D2 draft described baseline_dirty_snapshot as 'union of per-worker values'; it is a dispatch-time scalar copied verbatim by all workers (agents/dev.md:503) — unioning it could whitelist spurious paths
findings.proposed_fix: Require equality across workers; set aggregate value from orchestrator dispatch; mark blocked if any worker disagrees
findings.rationale: baseline_dirty_snapshot is not worker-owned data; it is a capture of pre-dispatch repo state. Only dev.* list fields (files_modified, files_created, observed_preexisting, etc.) should be unioned.

findings.issue: D2 must update both the summary union rule (commands/dev.md:699) and the detailed construction rule (commands/dev.md:720-734), not just the detailed section
findings.rationale: Both the summary and detailed sections serve as authoritative documentation; patching only one creates an inconsistency. Both are now in files_to_modify scope and Technical Hints.

findings.issue: D3 instruction 'minus baseline_dirty_snapshot entries' is ambiguous because baseline_dirty_snapshot is raw porcelain, not a path array
findings.proposed_fix: Define baseline_dirty_paths as paths parsed from the porcelain output; derivation formula uses that parsed set
findings.rationale: Concreteness is required here to avoid implementation ambiguity. AC3 and Technical Hints now reference 'baseline_dirty_paths (paths extracted from baseline_dirty_snapshot porcelain output)' explicitly.

findings.issue: Iter-2: AC5 AND clause used bare 'absent from files_modified' without union form — inconsistent with M1 and I2 fix
findings.proposed_fix: Update AC5 AND clause: 'AND if a different file other.md appears in diff and is absent from dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created and absent from baseline_dirty_paths but present in dev.observed_preexisting, QA DOES raise files_modified_underreport_violation for other.md'
findings.rationale: AC5 tested the observed_preexisting boundary but its wording still used the pre-normalization form. Fixed in iter-2 revision.

findings.issue: Iter-2: Evidence Expected text and context JSON requirement.success_criteria[0] still used bare 'absent from files_modified'
findings.proposed_fix: Update both to 'absent from dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created and absent from baseline_dirty_paths'
findings.rationale: Normative text outside AC blocks is also read by implementers; inconsistency creates drift risk.

findings.issue: Iter-2: acceptance-criteria JSON must have standard top-level envelope {request_id, task_id, generated_at, acceptance_criteria: [...]}
findings.proposed_fix: Create docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260524-125300-D.json with the correct envelope structure
findings.rationale: The envelope is required for downstream tooling that reads acceptance_criteria_path. File created with AC1-AC6.

findings.issue: Iter-2: context JSON files_to_create was [] but the AC JSON is a new BA artifact (not an implementation file); files_to_create must list it
findings.rationale: The development_approach section must accurately declare the file set created by this cycle.

findings.issue: Iter-2 OBSERVATION_ONLY: AC6 as proposed precisely tests the I1 union-exclusion boundary (diff path in files_created but not files_modified must not raise). AC1 provides the positive-control case. No additional edge case is required.
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed AC6 is correctly scoped. Optional hardening (second diff path absent from both lists) noted but not required.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Iter-1: all 7 findings applied (observed_preexisting exclusion removed, non-disjoint note added, observed_preexisting position corrected, baseline mismatch changed to block, scalar fields not unioned, both commands/dev.md locations patched, porcelain parsing made explicit). Iter-2: all 4 PROPOSED_FIX findings applied (AC5 AND clause normalized to union form, Evidence Expected and success_criteria[0] normalized, AC JSON created with proper envelope, files_to_create updated). 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY logged.
